name: sgmllib3k
documentation:
==================================================
sgmllib3k -- Py3k port of the old stdlib module
==================================================

sgmllib was dropped in Python 3. For those depending on it, that's somewhat unfortunate. This is a quick and dirty port of this old module. I just ran 2to3 on it and published it. I don't intend to maintain it, so it might be a good idea to eventually think about finding another module to use.

modules.name: sgmllib
documentation: A parser for SGML, using the derived class as a static DTD.

classes.name: sgmllib.SGMLParseError
documentation: Exception raised for all parse errors.

classes.name: sgmllib.SGMLParser

methods.name: sgmllib.SGMLParser.__init__
documentation: Initialize and reset this instance.

methods.name: sgmllib.SGMLParser.reset
documentation: Reset this instance. Loses all unprocessed data.

methods.name: sgmllib.SGMLParser.setnomoretags
documentation:
Enter literal mode (CDATA) till EOF.

Intended for derived classes only.

methods.name: sgmllib.SGMLParser.setliteral
documentation:
Enter literal mode (CDATA).

Intended for derived classes only.

methods.name: sgmllib.SGMLParser.feed
documentation:
Feed some data to the parser.

        Call this as often as you want, with as little or as much text
        as you want (may include '
').  (This just saves the text,
        all the processing is done by goahead().)

methods.name: sgmllib.SGMLParser.close
documentation: Handle the remaining data.

methods.name: sgmllib.SGMLParser.goahead
documentation: Internal -- handle data as far as reasonable.  May leave state
and data to be processed by a subsequent call.  If 'end' is
true, force handling all data as if followed by EOF marker.

methods.name: sgmllib.SGMLParser.parse_pi
documentation: Internal -- parse processing instr, return length or -1 if not terminated

methods.name: sgmllib.SGMLParser.parse_starttag
documentation: Internal -- handle starttag, return length or -1 if not terminated

methods.name: sgmllib.SGMLParser._convert_ref
documentation: Internal -- convert entity or character reference

methods.name: sgmllib.SGMLParser.parse_endtag
documentation: Internal -- parse endtag

methods.name: sgmllib.SGMLParser.finish_shorttag
documentation: Internal -- finish parsing of <tag/data/ (same as <tag>data</tag>)

methods.name: sgmllib.SGMLParser.finish_starttag
documentation: Internal -- finish processing of start tag
Return -1 for unknown tag, 0 for open-only tag, 1 for balanced tag

methods.name: sgmllib.SGMLParser.finish_endtag
documentation: Internal -- finish processing of end tag

methods.name: sgmllib.SGMLParser.handle_starttag
documentation: Overridable -- handle start tag

methods.name: sgmllib.SGMLParser.handle_endtag
documentation: Overridable -- handle end tag

methods.name: sgmllib.SGMLParser.report_unbalanced
documentation: Example -- report an unbalanced </...> tag.

methods.name: sgmllib.SGMLParser.convert_charref
documentation: Convert character reference, may be overridden.

methods.name: sgmllib.SGMLParser.handle_charref
documentation: Handle character reference, no need to override.

methods.name: sgmllib.SGMLParser.convert_entityref
documentation:
Convert entity references.

As an alternative to overriding this method; one can tailor the
results by setting up the self.entitydefs mapping appropriately.

methods.name: sgmllib.SGMLParser.handle_entityref
documentation: Handle entity references, no need to override.

methods.name: sgmllib.SGMLParser.handle_data
documentation: Example -- handle data, should be overridden

methods.name: sgmllib.SGMLParser.handle_comment
documentation: Example -- handle comment, could be overridden

methods.name: sgmllib.SGMLParser.handle_decl
documentation: Example -- handle declaration, could be overridden

methods.name: sgmllib.SGMLParser.handle_pi
documentation: Example -- handle processing instruction, could be overridden

methods.name: sgmllib.SGMLParser.unknown_starttag
documentation: To be overridden -- handlers for unknown objects
SGML parser base class -- find tags and call handler functions.
Usage: p = SGMLParser(); p.feed(data); ...; p.close().
The dtd is defined by deriving a class which defines methods
with special names to handle tags: start_foo and end_foo to handle
<foo> and </foo>, respectively, or do_foo to handle <foo> by itself.
(Tags are converted to lower case for this purpose.)  The data
between tags is passed to the parser by calling self.handle_data()
with some data as argument (the data may be split up in arbitrary
chunks).  Entity references are passed by calling
self.handle_entityref() with the entity reference as argument.
